title: Light Rays
description: OGL WebGL atmospheric light-ray effect emanating from a configurable origin point, with a soft volumetric glow that can be made mouse-reactive.
prompt_text:
# Light Rays

OGL WebGL atmospheric light-ray effect emanating from a configurable origin point, with a soft volumetric glow that can be made mouse-reactive.

## Use when
- Creating a god-ray or spotlight atmosphere on a dark landing page hero.
- Adding volumetric depth to a product showcase section.
- Building a calming, premium ambient background with directional light.

## Stack
- Framework: react
- Libraries: ogl

## Install
```bash
npm install ogl
```

## Component — light-rays.tsx
```tsx
// Source: animations_013.md
// Export: LightRays (default)
// Props: className, style, color (string), rayCount (number), rayLength (number),
//   rayWidth (number), origin ({x: number, y: number}), followMouse (boolean)
// Uses OGL Renderer, Program, Mesh, Triangle, Vec2, Vec3
// GLSL fragment shader for radial light rays with blur and opacity falloff
// See full source in raw/animations_013.md
```

## Notes
- Requires OGL (~100 KB). Lazy-load for performance.
